results.possibleIdentity: Dan Dubin was an electronics engineer based in Rochester, New York, during the late 1960s and 1970s. He was employed by Xerox Corporation and was active as a technical collaborator with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization established in 1966 to foster collaborations between artists and engineers. His professional profile, recorded in E.A.T.'s technical index, identifies his primary field as electronics engineering. Dubin resided in Rochester, which was a significant hub for Xerox's engineering and research activities during that era.
